name: Raised Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together 2 cups of flour, sugar, salt, and yeast.
2. Add the milk, shortening, and eggs to the dry ingredients. Beat on low speed for 1 minute, scraping the sides of the bowl frequently.
3. Increase the speed to medium and beat for another minute, continuing to scrape the bowl.
4. Stir in the remaining flour until a smooth dough forms. Cover and let the dough rise in a warm place for 50 to 60 minutes, or until doubled in size (the dough is ready if an indentation remains when touched).
5. Turn the dough onto a generously floured surface and roll it out lightly with your hands.
6. Flatten the dough using your hands or a rolling pin to about 1/2-inch thickness. Cut into rounds using a floured doughnut cutter.
7. Gather scraps, flatten slightly, and gently knead 2 or 3 times. Roll out the remaining dough to about 1/2-inch thickness and cut into additional rounds with a floured 3-inch doughnut cutter.
8. Cover the doughnuts and let them rise for an additional 30 to 40 minutes, or until doubled in size.
9. Heat oil (1 1/2 to 2 inches) in a Dutch oven to 350F.
10. Carefully slide the doughnuts into the hot oil using a wide spatula. Fry for about 1 minute on each side, or until golden brown.
11. Remove the doughnuts carefully from the oil and drain on paper towels. Roll or shake in sugar.
YIELD: About 2 dozen doughnuts
